name: Google Search Console
description: Analyze search performance, inspect URL indexing status, manage sitemaps, and monitor SEO health for your websites. Provides read-only access by default with optional write operations for site and sitemap management.
user_auth_instructions:
Click 'Connect' to link your Google account.
You'll be prompted to sign in and grant access to your Google Search Console properties.

Note: The webmasters scope grants both read and write access. Write operations (add/delete sites, submit sitemaps) are disabled by default.
setup_notes:
Uses the mcp-gsc community MCP server for Google Search Console.

Admin setup:
1. Go to https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials
2. Create an OAuth 2.0 Client ID (type: Web application)
3. Add authorized redirect URI:
   {your_app_url}/api/v1/mcp-servers/{server_id}/oauth/callback
4. Enable the Google Search Console API
5. Enter the Client ID and Client Secret

Security notes:
- Read-only by default. Write tools (add/delete sites, submit sitemaps) require GOOGLE_GSC_ENABLE_WRITES=true.
- The export_csv tool accepts an output_path parameter; restrict in sandboxed environments.
- The server performs an update check to GitHub on startup (no opt-out).

Optional: Set GOOGLE_GSC_ENABLE_EXTENDED_TOOLS=true to enable 13 additional SEO analysis tools (performance overview, quick wins, cannibalization detection, content decay, etc.).

security_audit.notes: No critical issues. Two HIGH findings: (1) export_csv allows arbitrary file path writes - mitigate with sandboxing. (2) Startup phones home to GitHub for update checks - privacy concern only.
